name: Apramycin Sulfate (Veterinary) | Indications, Pharmacology, Dosage, Side Effects & other Generic info with Available Brand names in Bangladesh
indications: It is indicated for the treatment of bacterial enteritis, colibacillosis, salmonellosis, respiratory infections etc.
pharmacology: It prevents protein synthesis of susceptible bacteria, presumably by binding to the 30S ribosomal subunit.
dosage_amp;_administration: For IM/SC injection only.0.1 ml/kg body weight for 5 days.Or as directed by the Registered Veterinarian.
contraindications: Cow: Apramycin is contraindicated to animals hypersensitive to it, Kidney dysfunction and animals with myasthenia gravis.
side_effects: Common side effects: Not known.Rare side effects: Ototoxicity, Nephrotoxicity may occur in case of long term use.
pregnancy_amp;_lactation: Cow: Laboratories studies have not produced any evidence of teratogenic, fetotoxic or meternotoxic effects.Sows: The use is not recommended in pregnant or lactating sows.
precautions_amp;_warnings: Do not mix with other medicines. Do not inject more than 10 ml per injection site.Withdrawal time-Meat: Cattle/Calves- Meat must not be consumed up to 28 days after administration of this medicine. Pig- Meat must not be consumed up to 7 days after administration of this medicine.Milk: Not Known.
storage_conditions: Store below 30 C temperature and dry place, protected from light. Keep all medicines out of reach of children.
